docs.text: OptiHeat Project Welcome to the OptiHeat Project website. OptiHeat is a collaboration between the Univerity of Bristol and Bristol City Council, with the aim of supporting social housing providers that want to deliver heat pumps into their housing stock. During the Spring of 2026 we will be running a series of event across the UK. This website also contains a repository of the resources that we have found and produced, all of which are free to download. Heatpump information for social housing providers Across the UK housing providers are making the switch the heatpumps. As part of the OptiHeat project we have created resources to support this transition. Supporting the Process We have broken out resources down into four main steps, that reflect the steps that housing provider has to go to when installing heatpumps. These are: Pre Install Design Installation Post Install This website is also structred in this manner to help you find the resources that are most relevent to you. Disclaimer This project is best endevours. We have tried to produce material that will be useful, but we cannot take any responsbility for decisions that are made based on the information given here. This is a fast moving field, and although we will do our best to keep resources up to date, we cannot make any guarantees on this.
docs.title: Home

docs.title: Tools to help

docs.text: Heat Pump Manurfacturers Remote Assistance We believe the heat pump remote monitoring (which we prefer to call "remote assistance") has a big role to play in improving the delivery of heat pumps across social housing, as well as saving social land lords considerable cost in the form of reduced call outs. For this reason we have been carrying out a review of the different remote assistance technologies provided by heat pump manurfactuers. This has been collated to produce the remote assistance disemination material that can be found on the resources page. We have tried to contact as many heat pump manurfacturers, or their party heat pump remote monitoring compainies as we can, but we are aware that there are others out there that we have not yet been able to talk to. If you are a heat pump manurfacturer that would be like to contriubute to this project, and get your remote assistance offering included in our material, then please complete the webform that is linked to below. We will then try and get in touch to arrange an interview. Remote Assistance Online Form
docs.title: Manufacturers

docs.text: We believe the heat pump remote monitoring (which we prefer to call "remote assistance") has a big role to play in improving the delivery of heat pumps across social housing, as well as saving social land lords considerable cost in the form of reduced call outs. For this reason we have been carrying out a review of the different remote assistance technologies provided by heat pump manurfactuers. This has been collated to produce the remote assistance disemination material that can be found on the resources page. We have tried to contact as many heat pump manurfacturers, or their party heat pump remote monitoring compainies as we can, but we are aware that there are others out there that we have not yet been able to talk to. If you are a heat pump manurfacturer that would be like to contriubute to this project, and get your remote assistance offering included in our material, then please complete the webform that is linked to below. We will then try and get in touch to arrange an interview. Remote Assistance Online Form

docs.text: Resources All the resources shown here are free to download. Strategic Review Social Housing provider Heat Pump Strategic Review template (coming soon!) Quality Assurance BCC INTERNAL Air Source Heat Pump Guidance for officers and installers This document is intended as a guide to heat pump installation. It makes reference to but is not a substitution for industry standards set by bodies including MCS and the certification bodies, planning regulations and DNO procedures. It goes beyond some of the requirements of these regulations to ensure quality installations in Bristol. National Trust ASHP specification This Document describes the performance requirements for small scale Air Source Heat Pumps for the National Trust Let Estate. Broadacres ASHP specification This Document describes the specification requirements for Air Source Heat Pumps for the Broadacres Housing Assosiation. Social housing heat pump installation audit form This template audit form has been developed by Bristol City Council and Nesta to provide a clear process for auditing and quality assuring heat pumps installations in social housing. If you would like to repurpose this form, please read the instruction provided below. Social housing heat pump installation audit form instruction These are the instructions on how to use the template audit form provided above. ASHP Heat pump technical audit form This audit form was developed by Bristol City Council and Nesta to provide a clear process for auditing and quality assuring heat pumps installations in social housing. BCC heat pump repairs form This is a form to be used to record the outcomes of repair visits. BCC ASHP post installation 5 point check visit form This is a guide designed for non-technical staff when checking a heat pump installation. Heat Pump QA process map and metrics Very high level slides showing the process to heat pump installation and the metrics by which this process can be measured. Heat Pump QA process map and metrics data log A table for recording the heat pump process metrics.<!-- * Organisational heat pump strategic review outline process A document outlining the what should be included in a heat pump strategy. --> Post audit reporting protocol A document describing the protocol to carry out after a heat pump audit has raised issues. Training HVAC Education Hub A series of videos, delivered by Mario Dodić, giving a significant amount of information about heat pumps and how they should be designed, installed, controlled and maintained. Heat Geek A series of videos providing information about heat pumps. Handover Guidance CSE Heat Pump Advice leaflet A well produced leaflet, created by the Centre for Sustainable Energy, providing information about heatpumps. BCC CSE Tenant Advice Service heat pump advice for tenants A well produced leaflet providing information about heatpumps designed to be provided to tenants after they have had a heat pump installed. C3 Tenant ASHP short Handover document Nesta.docx A short document to give to tenants explaining how they can get the bets results from their heat pump. Set-by-step guides to tame and train your heat pump Jargon free pictorial guides taking you step by step through how to train and tame your heat pump. Remote Assistance Heat pump remote assistance privacy notice An example privacy notice conserning the data gathered by a heat pump remote assistance system. THIS IS TO BE USED AS AN EXAMPLE ONLY, AND SHOULD NOT BE SHARED WITH TENANTS UNTIL IT HAS BEEN APPROVED BY YOUR IN HOUSE LEGAL TEAM! Heat Pump Remote Assistance Disemination Material A guide to heat pump remote assistance systems and how they can benefit social housing landlords. Remote Assistance Manurfacturer Survey Results A spreadsheet contining the results of the survery into different manufacturers heat pump remote assistance offerings. This will be added to as more manurfacturers approve content for disemination. General Guidance MCS Domestic heat pumps - a best practice guide The standards and tools library provided by the MCS. Renewable Heating Hub A series of video providing information for home owners on the subject of heat pumps, sollar panels and house batteries. Planning for heat: what you need to know about heat pumps A webinar delivered by Nesta on the subject of heat pumps.
docs.title: Resources

docs.text: All the resources shown here are free to download.
